2605.30042.title: Learning to Choose: An Empowerment-Guided Multi-Agent System with semantic communication for Adaptive Method Selection
2605.30042.paper_summary: Automating scientific computing workflows requires more than generating executable code: autonomous systems must also select appropriate computational strategies, implement them faithfully, and ensure that the resulting outcomes remain causally attributable to the decisions that produced them. In multi-agent pipelines, this process is particularly fragile, as small inconsistencies between agent intentions and actions can lead to semantic drift, where the eventually executed procedure no longer reflects the originally selected strategy, thereby corrupting downstream evaluation and adaptation. In this work, motivated by the ATHENA framework (Toscano et al., 2025; Toscano et al., 2026) and the concept of empowerment (Yiu et al., 2025), we introduce a multi-agent framework that combines contextual bandits with structured inter-agent communication and, most importantly, semantic checkpoints that preserve action-outcome fidelity throughout the pipeline. The system integrates specialized large language model (LLM) agents, grounded code generation, and self-healing execution loops within an adaptive decision-making architecture. Interpreting the framework through the lens of empowerment, we show that reliable autonomous learning requires not only identifying high-quality actions, but also preserving the integrity of their propagation across agents. Using sensitivity analysis and uncertainty quantification workflows as representative case studies, we demonstrate that unchecked semantic drift degrades policy learning, whereas the proposed framework improves convergence, robustness, and adaptation to novel problem contexts. These results suggest a broader design principle for scientific multi-agent systems: adaptive decision-making must be coupled with explicit mechanisms that guarantee semantic consistency and reliable information flow across the computational pipeline.
2605.30042.paper_summary_zh: 自動化科學計算工作流程不僅需要生成可執行的代碼：自主系統還必須選擇適當的計算策略，忠實地實施它們，並確保最終結果能夠因果地歸因於產生它們的決策。在多代理管道中，這一過程特別脆弱，因為代理意圖和行動之間的小不一致可能導致語義漂移，最終執行的程序不再反映最初選擇的策略，從而破壞下游的評估和適應。在本研究中，受ATHENA框架（Toscano et al., 2025; Toscano et al., 2026）和賦權概念（Yiu et al., 2025）的啟發，我們介紹了一個多代理框架，該框架結合了上下文強盜、結構化的代理間通信，最重要的是，語義檢查點，這些檢查點在整個管道中保持行動-結果的真實性。該系統集成了專門的大型語言模型（LLM）代理、基於實體的代碼生成和自我修復執行循環，並融入了一個自適應決策架構。通過賦權的視角解釋該框架，我們表明可靠的自主學習不僅需要識別高質量的行動，還需要保持它們在代理之間傳播的完整性。使用敏感性分析和不確定性量化工作流程作為代表性案例研究，我們證明未受控制的語義漂移會降低政策學習，而所提出的框架則改善了收斂性、穩健性和對新問題情境的適應性。這些結果暗示了科學多代理系統的一個更廣泛的設計原則：自適應決策必須與明確的機制結合，以保證語義一致性和可靠的信息流通過計算管道。
2605.30042.author: Geremy Loachamín-Suntaxi et.al.
2605.30042.authors: Geremy Loachamín-Suntaxi, Robert Lazar, Dimitrios G. Giovanis, Ioannis G. Kevrekidis, Eleni D. Koronaki
